fresh_facts.claim: US military operations have conducted over 7,000 strikes against Iranian targets, degrading Iran's military capabilities by 90-95% in key areas as of April 10, 2026.
More than 800 US strikes occurred on Tuesday night before a two-week ceasefire, destroying 80% of Iran's air defenses, 90% of the navy fleet, and 95% of naval mines.
Precision strikes hit over 90 targets on Kar Island, destroying air defenses, naval bases, and mine facilities.
Seven Iranian UAVs were intercepted in Kuwaiti airspace within 24 hours as of April 11, 2026, causing damage and injuries.
A US MQ-4C Triton drone vanished in the Strait of Hormuz hours before ceasefire talks.
Iran's Supreme National Security Council agreed to a two-week ceasefire if US strikes halt, but vowed full force response to any enemy error.
Iran dismissed Trump's 15-point ceasefire plan as 'deceptive'.
Iran drew a red line against Israeli strikes on Beirut, prompting US pressure to scale back attacks.
Iran's military ballistic missile and UAV attacks have decreased by 90% since the conflict start.
Iran's air defenses are described as 'flattened' by US military operations.
US military strikes have degraded Iran's defense manufacturing capabilities by 90% according to Pentagon reports.
US military operations include A-10 Warthog strikes on southern Iran and AH-64 Apache attacks on Iranian-aligned militias in Iraq.
US military strikes on April 10, 2026 included 5,000 lb penetrator bombs targeting underground coastal defense missile storage.
Trump delayed power plant attacks amid ceasefire talks with Iran.
US President Trump issued a 'final' deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, tied to economic pressures.
US and Iranian envoys are set to meet in Pakistan for diplomatic negotiations over the weekend of April 12-13, 2026.
Disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz have caused economic shocks affecting global oil and gas prices and trade.
Recent protests in Iran represent the most serious domestic unrest in Islamic Republic history, threatening the regime's character.
US and Israeli operations in Iran raise terrorism and nuclear proliferation concerns amid an asymmetrical conflict with no clear end in sight.
Iran leverages control of the Strait of Hormuz to apply economic pressure amid global inflation concerns.
Reports indicate Iran has diverted resources to missiles and proxy forces rather than addressing domestic needs.

regime_fall.current_status: Following over 7,000 US strikes that degraded Iran's military capabilities by 90-95%, a ceasefire negotiated in early April 2026 collapsed as Israeli strikes resumed in mid-June. The regime faces critical military losses, economic pressure from Strait of Hormuz disruptions, and unprecedented domestic unrest alongside intensifying internal security crackdowns.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Post-ceasefire repression surge including over 700 arrests, IRGC/Basij raids across Iranian cities, and fatal shootings in Khomein and Hamedan on July 2, 2026, indicating regime consolidating control through internal security operations amid military losses.
